title: Affordable Housing vs Parking
description: A city is debating whether to convert a public parking lot into affordable housing.

participants.name: Maya
participants.statement: We need affordable housing more than another parking lot. Public land should serve people who need homes.

participants.name: Jordan
participants.statement: Removing parking will hurt small businesses that rely on customer access.

participants.name: Luis
participants.statement: Housing near transit can reduce car dependency and help the climate.

participants.name: Evelyn
participants.statement: I support housing, but elderly residents still need accessible parking nearby.

participants.name: Priya
participants.statement: The city never listens to renters. Affordable homes should be the priority.

participants.name: Owen
participants.statement: Business owners are worried because customers need easy parking for short trips.

participants.name: Nora
participants.statement: A mixed-use building could include loading zones, accessible spaces, and better transit passes.

participants.name: Sam
participants.statement: Before removing parking, we need data on how full the lot is and who uses it.

participants.name: Aisha
participants.statement: Public land should address the housing crisis, not store private cars.

participants.name: Ben
participants.statement: The shops already struggle, and less parking would make the area harder to visit.

participants.name: Grace
participants.statement: Transit access matters, but the plan must protect disabled residents and caregivers.

participants.name: Marco
participants.statement: I support more homes if the city proves the parking impact will be managed.

participants.name: Mrs. Tanaka
participants.statement: I've owned my home on this block for 38 years. Whatever you build will affect my neighborhood for decades. Listen to long-time residents, not just new arrivals.

participants.name: Tyrell
participants.statement: I'm 24, working two jobs, sleeping on my brother's couch. Every month a new development opens with 'affordable units' that aren't actually affordable to me. Define what 'affordable' means before celebrating.

participants.name: Hassan
participants.statement: I drive for a rideshare. That parking lot is where I get coffee and use the restroom mid-shift. Drivers have nowhere else to go. Don't pretend gig workers don't exist in your planning.

participants.name: Father Russo
participants.statement: Our church is across the street. Sunday service brings 200 elderly worshippers who can't take stairs to street parking. The plan needs to address us specifically.
